description: Letter sent from Alexandria, in which the writer, Avraham b. Elazar the doctor, reports about the arrival of a ship from Marseilles, containing queries from a distant country to Maimonides (l. 28r), and complains about a new imposition by the Sultan and the general poverty of the local community. Dating: ca. 1168–1204 CE, based on the years when Maimonides was active in Egypt. (Information from Mediterranean Society, I, p. 301 and from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
